label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so the user is asking if a man can leave all his money to his widow's sister, who he plans to marry next year. Let me break this down step by step. First, the man is currently married, and he's leaving his money to his widow's sister. Wait, but he's alive and plans to marry someone else next year. So, the person he's leaving the money to is the sister of his current wife. That sister is not his step-sister yet, but his future wife's sister. But he's not married to that sister yet—he's going to marry the current wife's sister in the future. But here's the catch: when making a will, the person has to be alive and have the capacity to make the will. The man is alive now, and he's making a provision in his will to leave money to someone who he intends to marry. But the key here is whether the legal system recognizes such
